channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Paraná Pesquisas: Flávio Bolsonaro cresce no Ceará, mas segue quase 20 pontos atrás de Lula
item.description: Lula leads with 52.7% in the first round and 55.3% in the second round in Ceará, while Flávio Bolsonaro trails at 29.7% and 33.7%, respectively. Despite a modest 2.4-point gain for Bolsonaro since March, Lula’s lead remains between 17.8 and 23 points in the first round and 16.4 to 21.6 in the second, within the margin of error. Other candidates perform insignificantly, all below 2.4%.

item.title: AtlasIntel: 86,7% dizem já ter voto decidido para outubro
item.description: The AtlasIntel poll shows that 86.7% of Brazilians have already decided their presidential vote for October 2026, with 63.6% expressing enthusiasm. The survey, conducted March 16–23, 2026, interviewed 4,224 people with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: A estratégia de Pacheco para emplacar Messias no STF
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB) plans to meet with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre to expedite the analysis of Jorge Messias' nomination to the Supreme Federal Court (STF) by President Lula. Pacheco aims to overcome Alcolumbre's resistance, who prefers Pacheco for the STF seat and learned of the presidential message only through the press, further straining their relationship. There is expectation that Alcolumbre will delay the nomination in the Senate's Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ), prompting Pacheco to intervene politically to unblock the process.

item.title: As novas campanhas publicitárias do governo Lula
item.description: The Lula government plans to launch two advertising campaigns in the second half of April to improve its polling numbers. Coordinated by Secom, the initiative aims to bolster the president's image amid declining popularity. Full access to the content is restricted to subscribers of the outlet that published the report.

item.title: Após Alckmin e Tabata, PSB amplia refúgio para liberais pró-Lula com filiação de Tebet e Pacheco
item.description: The PSB is expanding its base by incorporating liberal Lula-allied leaders such as Simone Tebet and Rodrigo Pacheco, following the earlier inclusion of Geraldo Alckmin and Tabata Amaral. This strategy aims to strengthen the party as a refuge for government allies who do not wish to join the PT, maintaining relevance in the progressive camp amid the rise of Bolsonarism. The recruitment of figures from MDB, DEM, União Brasil, and PSD reflects a party realignment driven by current political circumstances.

item.title: As ideias do novo ministro da Fazenda
item.description: The new Finance Minister, Dario Durigan, announced that the upcoming household debt reduction program will include debt discounts, government guarantees, and be simpler than the Desenrola initiative. He noted that over 15 million people have been banked during the current Lula administration. Durigan also criticized the rhetoric of some young leaders as 'little republican,' reaffirming his commitment to fiscal rules, a free election, and acceptance of results as democratic essentials.

item.title: Política no STF
item.description: The nomination of Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court (STF) is criticized for being based on personal loyalty to President Lula rather than judicial merit, as the requirement of 'notable legal knowledge' is no longer considered in the selection process. The article argues that this practice undermines the Senate’s approval ritual, turning it into a political favor regardless of the president’s ideological line.

item.title: Programa contra inadimplência sai em 10 dias, diz Durigan
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan announced that the government will launch a new anti-delinquency program within 10 days, aimed at simplifying debt renegotiation and incorporating financial education to prevent recurring indebtedness. Unlike the 2023 Desenrola initiative, this program will involve direct negotiations with banks, fintechs, and credit operators, offering discounts and government guarantees (not subsidies) to reduce financial institutions' risk. Durigan also ruled out federal intervention in the Bank of Brasília and said reducing the IOF for low-income debtors is under analysis, though he initially opposes the measure.

item.title: Governo pagou menos de 1% das emendas que precisa quitar até junho pelo calendário aprovado na Lei do Orçamento
item.description: By the end of March, the Lula government had paid only R$102.3 million in mandatory amendments for 2026, equivalent to 0.6% of the R$17.3 billion required for the first half of the year. Payments were exclusively for SUAS-related actions, benefiting 160 individual amendments from deputies and senators, with the PL and Republicans receiving the largest shares.

item.title: Estratégia eleitoral de Lula esbarra no caso Master e expõe as armadilhas do autoengano
item.description: Lula met with ministers who will run in the October elections to energize his re-election campaign, urging them to 'go after' Flávio Bolsonaro and claiming his government has achieved 'infinitely more' than the previous one. Rui Costa, from the Civil House, presented a comparison between Lula and Bolsonaro, describing the current administration as a 'change from water to wine,' and criticized Sidônio Palmeira of Communications for not doing his job properly. The article is subscriber-only, requiring registration for full access.

item.title: Todo mundo odeia o Lula?
item.description: Lula faces political isolation in Congress and the market, yet maintains strong popular appeal through concrete policies like income tax exemptions for workers, the Pé-de-Meia program, and taxing the super-rich. Despite the lack of a competitive center in 2026, voters prioritize economic and quality-of-life issues over ideological clashes, which could benefit Lula if he doubles down on what is already perceived as effective. The real divide is not between conservatism and progressivism, but between traditional social values and demands for economic justice and material security.

item.title: Condecorada, chefe de hospitais e pediatra: conheça a trajetória da primeira general mulher no Exército brasileiro
item.description: Pediatrician Claudia Lima Gusmão Cacho became the first woman to reach the rank of general in the Brazilian Army, following a formal ceremony at the Army Club in Brasília where she received the general's sword and command baton. The promotion was signed by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) after a secret vote by the High Command.

item.title: Messias amplia apoio, mas ainda não tem votos para indicação ser aprovada em comissão do Senado; veja placar
item.description: Attorney General Jorge Messias has gained support in the Senate's CCJ for his STF nomination, securing at least ten favorable votes, but still falls short of the 14 needed for approval. Six senators have declared opposition, and eleven remain undecided or unresponsive, leaving the outcome uncertain. While backed by the government base and factions of MDB and PSD, resistance from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre and indecision among others continue to hinder consensus.

item.title: Fala de Lula sobre senadores pensarem 'que são Deus' amplia desafio de Messias para ter maioria no CCJ
item.description: Lula stated that senators 'think they are God' in an interview, heightening tensions with Congress, particularly following prior strains with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre. The remark came on the same day Jorge Messias' nomination to the STF was formally submitted, though he still lacks sufficient votes for approval in the CCJ, despite gaining supporters since last year. The comment caused discomfort among Centrão leaders, complicating efforts to secure a majority for the nomination.

item.title: Grupo de Paes no PSD articula candidatura à presidência da Alerj e tenta ampliar bancada
item.description: The Eduardo Paes-aligned faction within the PSD is positioning to launch its own candidate for president of the Alerj and aims to grow its caucus from six to ten deputies, seeking to become the second-largest bloc in the assembly. The party is negotiating the affiliation of figures such as Célia Jordão, Vinicius Cozzolino, Renato Miranda, and André Correa, while considering Vitor Júnior and Correa as potential leaders. The final decision awaits the STF ruling on April 8, after which the PSD will confirm its stance.

item.title: Ministros do STF se mobilizam por Messias; aliados de Alcolumbre veem risco de derrota para Lula
item.description: STF ministers are mobilizing to approve Jorge Messias's nomination to the Supreme Court, viewing it as strategically important to prevent post-electoral instability and strengthen the Court amid a vacant seat. Allies of Senate President Davi Alcolumbre maintain neutrality, fearing the opposition—led by Flávio Bolsonaro—could turn the confirmation into a political defeat for Lula with electoral consequences.

item.title: Brasil recluta a los bancos en su lucha para eliminar la deforestación en la Amazonia
item.description: The Brazilian federal government now requires banks to check, starting this week, whether rural properties applying for subsidized credit have a history of deforestation since 2019, using an official database. The measure supports the goal of completely eliminating deforestation in the Amazon by 2030, as pledged by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. While banks support the initiative, agribusiness producers have opposed it, viewing it as an excessive restriction.

item.title: Grandes distribuidoras de combustíveis decidem ficar fora de programa de subvenção ao diesel
item.description: Brazil's largest fuel distributors have decided not to participate in the first phase of the diesel subsidy program launched by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva to counter the impact of the Iran war on fuel prices. The companies cited economic and operational concerns about the program's viability, which aims to curb price increases through direct subsidies to the sector.

item.title: Pocotó, pocotó
item.description: The text critiques the rise of Ronaldo Caiado as a new political option in Brazil, questioning whether he truly represents a redeeming 'third way' capable of overcoming polarization or merely another repetition of choosing between bad and worse options. It suggests the country may be paying the 'karma' of past generations for accepting figures like Caiado as saviors, despite his platform not being clearly defined in the excerpt. The content is presented as exclusive subscriber material, requiring registration for full access.

item.title: Visão do passado arrisca reeleição
item.description: President Lula attributes his government's low approval ratings to poor communication about its achievements, blaming both the press for not adequately reporting on government actions and the Presidency's formal channels for failing to inform the public. He suggests the media should function as an extension of the Official Gazette, a view described as mistaken. The article is available only to subscribers.

item.title: Brasil tem a 65ª gasolina mais barata do mundo, segundo plataforma; veja ranking depois do início da guerra no Irã
item.description: Brazil ranks 65th in the world for most expensive gasoline, with an average price of $1.273 per liter (R$ 6.56), according to Global Petrol Prices. Despite rising global oil prices due to the Middle East conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran, Brazil remains among the least affected countries thanks to domestic production and tax policies. Libya, Iran, and Venezuela have the cheapest fuel, while Hong Kong has the most expensive gasoline globally.

item.title: Fluminense supera o Corinthians e se aproxima da liderança do Brasileiro
item.description: Fluminense defeated Corinthians 3-1 in the ninth round of the Brasileirão, with goals from John Kennedy, Hércules, and Castillo, reaching 19 points and moving into second place, tied with league leaders Palmeiras. Corinthians remained on 10 points in 11th place. Germán Cano returned to action in the final minutes after recovering from a knee sprain sustained during the 2026 preseason, receiving applause from the fans.

item.title: Caiado: “Vocês vão ver o que é jogar pesado contra bandido”
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, former governor of Goiás and PSD presidential pre-candidate, stated that if elected, he will 'play hard against criminals' to return Brazil to 'honest citizens', emphasizing his zero-tolerance stance on crime and commitment to protecting the population.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro e Lula empatam em São Paulo, mas senador leva a vantagem em 2º turno
item.description: In São Paulo, Flávio Bolsonaro and Lula are technically tied in voting intentions for the 2026 presidency, but the senator leads in runoff simulations. The data indicates a competitive scenario in the country's largest electoral college, with a potential advantage for the Bolsonaro-aligned candidate in a direct confrontation.

item.title: Como tirar a segunda via do título de eleitor pelo celular em 2026 sem sair de casa em poucos minutos
item.description: In 2026, voters can obtain a second copy of their voter ID via mobile phone without visiting an electoral office, in just a few minutes. The process is fully online through the official TSE website or app. This initiative aims to simplify electoral regularization, especially ahead of key deadlines.

item.title: Presidida por trumpista, Comissão na Câmara dos EUA cita eleições no Brasil e acusa Moraes de 'censura'
item.description: The US House Judiciary Committee, chaired by Republican Jim Jordan, released a report accusing Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes of leading a 'censorship campaign' that threatens free speech in the US and Brazil. The document claims Moraes targets speeches by Brazilian journalists and commentators in the US and argues his rulings against the Bolsonaro family could hinder their online expression ahead of Brazil’s 2026 presidential election. It also references the electoral contest between Flávio Bolsonaro and Lula as context for the 'lawfare' allegations.

item.title: PM relata ao STF troca do carregador da tornozeleira de Bolsonaro
item.description: The Military Police of the Federal District informed the STF that it replaced the charger of Bolsonaro's electronic ankle monitor between 12:34 a.m. and 1:03 a.m. on March 29. The replacement was carried out by prison officer Rita de Cassia Gaio, who had previously intervened when Bolsonaro attempted to tamper with the device using a soldering iron. Bolsonaro is serving a 90-day house arrest sentence for his conviction in the coup plot case and must keep the device charged 24 hours a day.

item.title: Adversária em 2024, Cristina Graeml se filia ao PSD e passa a integrar grupo político de Ratinho Jr.
item.description: Journalist Cristina Graeml announced her affiliation with the PSD, the party of Paraná Governor Ratinho Jr., in a video posted on social media. She acknowledged having been an opponent of Ratinho Jr. in the 2024 Curitiba municipal election but defended the need to build alliances for a larger project. The PSD is her fourth party in less than a year, following stints in the PMB, Podemos, and União Brasil.

item.title: Pacheco chega ao PSB e fala em romper “sucateamento da máquina” em MG
item.description: Former Senate president Rodrigo Pacheco (ex-PSD-MG) joined the PSB on April 1, 2026, aiming to run for governor of Minas Gerais and build a platform for President Lula. In his speech, he criticized Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) for the 'dismantling of the public machine' and called for a 'reconstruction' of the state focused on progress, development, and public servant valorization. Pacheco also emphasized his lifelong commitment to democracy, referencing his role during the pandemic and the January 8 events.

item.title: Dino afirma que CNJ tem papel 'insubstituível' no debate ético após revisão de penduricalhos
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Flávio Dino stated that the National Council of Justice (CNJ) plays an 'irreplaceable' role in judicial ethics, drawing from his past experience as the council's General Secretary. He urged the CNJ to advance on issues such as judicial allowances (penduricalhos) and compulsory retirement, advocating for the implementation of STF rulings and the drafting of a national law to regulate judicial compensation. Dino also called on CNJ President Edson Fachin to reform the judiciary's disciplinary system, replacing compulsory dismissal with effective sanctions for judges who commit serious crimes or ethical violations.

item.title: Pacheco se filia ao PSB, mas ainda não crava disputa ao Governo de Minas
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco officially joined the PSB in Brasília, emphasizing the defense of democracy and signaling a potential run for governor of Minas Gerais. His goal is to strengthen Lula’s electoral base in the state, though he has not yet confirmed his candidacy.

item.title: Rodrigo Pacheco se filia ao PSB de Alckmin de olho na disputa pelo governo de MG
item.description: Former Senate President Rodrigo Pacheco joined the Brazilian Socialist Party (PSB) on Wednesday night, January 1st, aiming to launch his pre-candidacy for governor of Minas Gerais, with expected support from President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT). The move is part of a political strategy to build a competitive ticket in the state for the 2026 elections.

item.title: Congresso aprova guarda compartilhada de pets
item.description: Congress has approved a bill establishing shared pet custody in cases of separation, either through mutual agreement or judicial determination of balanced custody and expense division when no agreement is reached. The measure responds to rising legal disputes over pets, reflecting a societal shift where animals are viewed as family members, particularly in a country with over 164 million pets and declining birth rates. The law excludes cases involving domestic violence history or risk and is expected to be signed by President Lula.

item.title: Rodrigo Pacheco oficializa filiação ao PSB, mas evita cravar candidatura
item.description: Rodrigo Pacheco officially joined the PSB in an event attended by Geraldo Alckmin and João Campos, but declined to confirm a candidacy for governor of Minas Gerais. Despite expectations from the PT and the party’s national leadership, he stated that his affiliation is not an electoral decision act. Pacheco, seen as a moderate figure with broad municipal outreach, is considered strategically important for an alliance with Lula in Minas Gerais.

item.title: Pacheco se filia ao PSB “com nove anos de atraso”
item.description: Rodrigo Pacheco, former president of the PSD, officially joined the Brazilian Socialist Party (PSB) in an event in Brasília, aligning himself more closely with President Lula’s (PT) government. The move is seen as a strategic step to strengthen his political base ahead of a potential gubernatorial run in Minas Gerais in 2026. O Antagonista highlighted the affiliation with irony, noting Pacheco arrived 'nine years late' relative to when his ideological trajectory would have naturally aligned with the party.

item.title: Pacheco se filia ao PSB e deixa candidatura ao governo de Minas em aberto: ‘Definições ainda virão’
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco officially joined the Brazilian Socialist Party (PSB) in Belo Horizonte, leaving his potential candidacy for governor of Minas Gerais in 2026 undecided. Despite explicit support from President Lula and the presence of leaders like João Campos and Geraldo Alckmin, Pacheco stressed that decisions must emerge from dialogue with Minas Gerais political actors, not from Brasília. He emphasized the need for a different path from the current 'dismantling of public administration' in the state.

item.title: Com fim de janela partidária, PSD de Kassab passa a ter 3ª bancada na Alesp; PSDB cai para 10ª
item.description: Following the end of the party-switching window in São Paulo’s Legislative Assembly (Alesp), Gilberto Kassab’s PSD grew from 4 to 11 deputies, becoming the third-largest caucus, while the PSDB dropped from 8 to 2 deputies, falling from fourth to tenth place. PL and Republicanos each gained two deputies, strengthening Tarcísio de Freitas’ base, whereas Cidadania, PDT, and Rede lost all representation. Most deputies (82%) plan to run for re-election in the October state elections.

item.title: ‘Homem gestante’: licença-paternidade foi recebida com piada na Constituinte de 1988
item.description: In 1988, the proposal for paternity leave in the Constituent Assembly was met with jokes and mockery by deputies, reflecting a machista culture that viewed child-rearing as solely the woman's responsibility. Despite the resistance, paternity leave was initially approved at five days, and now, nearly 40 years later, President Lula has signed a law to gradually expand it.

item.title: Rodrigo Pacheco filia-se ao PSB e busca apoio de Lula para disputar governo de Minas
item.description: Rodrigo Pacheco joined the PSB in Brasília to run for governor of Minas Gerais in 2026, seeking Lula’s support. The affiliation took place with the presence of Geraldo Alckmin, João Campos, and Márcio França, marking his exit from the PSD. The move aims to strengthen his pre-candidacy in a PT-aligned party, easing alignment with the governing coalition.

item.title: Influenciador diz que foi detido pela PF em ato de Lula, após chamar o presidente de ladrão
item.description: Influencer Pedro Arthur, with over 200,000 Instagram followers, was detained by the Federal Police during a Lula event in Ceará after calling the president a 'thief', 'criminal', and 'vagabond'. Known for attacking left-wing politicians in the region, he stated in videos that his goal is to 'end the PT in the Northeast' and appeared alongside pre-candidates from the Mission party. Neither the PF nor Lula’s office confirmed the detention at the time of publication.

item.title: Desgaste de imagem forçará Lula a mudar postura cautelosa
item.description: The war in the Middle East is driving up energy and fertilizer costs, estimated at R$20 billion over the next four months, forcing Lula’s government to implement emergency measures like tax renouncements and subsidies. However, these expenses compound existing household debt, which has eroded the impact of benefits such as the income tax exemption up to R$5,000, fueling electoral distrust. Efforts to use subsidized credit to relieve debt risk being redirected toward additional consumption, worsening the crisis and reinforcing the perception that conditions are deteriorating.

item.title: Tarcísio autoriza construção de centro para PcD vítimas de violência em São Paulo
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas authorized the construction of a center in São Paulo city to provide integrated care for people with disabilities who are victims of violence. The facility will offer multidisciplinary support, including health, social assistance, and rights protection.

item.title: Cotado para disputar o governo de Minas Gerais, senador Rodrigo Pacheco se filia ao PSB
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB-MG) officially joined the Brazilian Socialist Party (PSB) in Brasília, highlighting the party's long history and commitment to fighting authoritarianism. The move aligns him with President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's strategy to support Lula's re-election bid in Minas Gerais, a key electoral state. Pacheco is being considered as a potential candidate for governor of Minas Gerais in 2026 to strengthen federal-state alliances.

item.title: ‘Vocês são as pessoas honestas que querem que eu seja’, diz Lula, em ato falho, no Ceará
item.description: President Lula made a verbal slip while speaking to youth in Ceará, telling them they 'are the honest people I want to be,' amid a discussion about young people's belief that all politicians are corrupt. He urged them not to give up on politics, emphasizing that everyone can take responsibility, despite widespread skepticism.

item.title: Otto Alencar mantém Weverton Rocha, alvo da PF, como relator da sabatina de Messias no STF
item.description: Senate CCJ President Otto Alencar kept Weverton Rocha as rapporteur for Jorge Messias' STF confirmation hearing, despite Rocha being targeted in the PF's Operation Sem Desconto. The presidential nomination was sent Wednesday, but awaits scheduling by Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, facing potential rejection due to opposition mobilization.

item.title: Governo federal faz mais duas trocas em ministérios antes das eleições
item.description: Environment Minister Marina Silva and Transport Minister Renan Filho formally resigned from their federal cabinet positions on Wednesday to run in the October elections, with Silva seeking a Senate seat in São Paulo and Filho aiming for the governorship of Alagoas. They are being replaced by their respective executive secretaries, João Paulo Capobianco and George Palermo Santoro. Approximately 18 of President Lula’s 37 ministers are expected to step down to run for office, complying with Brazil’s electoral incompatibility rule, which requires public officials to leave their posts up to six months before elections — a deadline that falls on April 4.

item.title: Lindbergh aciona STF e Conselho de Ética contra Alfredo Gaspar
item.description: Federal Deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ) filed a criminal complaint with the STF and an ethics complaint with the Chamber’s Council of Ethics against colleague Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL) for crimes against honor and breach of parliamentary decorum, following Gaspar’s unsubstantiated accusation during the INSS CPMI that Lindbergh had raped a minor. Lindbergh states he acted as a citizen and congressman by forwarding information received from journalists through legal and institutional channels.

item.title: Comitê dos EUA critica Moraes e mira eleições de 2026
item.description: The U.S. House Judiciary Committee released a report criticizing Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes for allegedly restricting freedom of expression in Brazil. Titled 'The Attack on Free Speech Abroad: The Case of Brazil,' it is the third in a series examining judicial overreach. The report implies that such actions could affect the political climate ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Leite e Caiado conversam por telefone após ruído no PSD
item.description: Eduardo Leite, governor of Rio Grande do Sul, called Ronaldo Caiado on Tuesday night, the 31st, amid internal PSD tension following Leite's public remarks against Caiado's presidential pre-candidacy. The call came days after the PSD formally named Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate, indicating efforts to ease party discord. The contact suggests a dialogue attempt despite recent disagreements.

item.title: Em busca de delação, advogado de Zettel se reúne com Mendonça
item.description: Fabiano Zettel, brother-in-law of banker Daniel Vorcaro and detained in Operation Compliance Zero over suspected fraud at Banco Master, is seeking a plea bargain deal. His new defense team, led by lawyer Celso Vilardi — who previously defended Jair Bolsonaro and worked on cases like Lava Jato and Mensalão — met with Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça to negotiate cooperation. The change in legal counsel followed disagreements over a plea deal strategy, given Zettel’s close ties to Vorcaro, his role as a major donor to Bolsonaro-aligned campaigns, and his potential to reveal key details about the alleged scheme involving false credit instruments and money laundering.
